d.description:
Standard editor line manipulation bindings:

- **Opt+Up / Opt+Down** — Move current line (or selected lines) up/down
- **Opt+Shift+Up / Opt+Shift+Down** — Duplicate line up/down
- **Cmd+Shift+D** — Duplicate line (alternative binding)
- **Cmd+Enter** — Insert blank line below cursor
- **Cmd+Shift+Enter** — Insert blank line above cursor
- **Cmd+J** — Join lines (merge next line into current)

Requires new command types: `moveLine`, `duplicateLine`, `insertLineBelow`, `insertLineAbove`, `joinLines`.
`deleteLine` (Cmd+Shift+K) is already implemented.
d.title: Line operations: move, duplicate, insert above/below

d.description:
Standard indentation bindings:

- **Tab** (with selection) — Indent selected lines
- **Shift+Tab** — Dedent selected lines (always, not just with selection)
- **Cmd+]** — Indent line/selection
- **Cmd+[** — Dedent line/selection
- **Auto-indent on Enter** — New line matches indentation of previous line

Currently Tab always inserts 2 spaces regardless of selection. Need to distinguish between:
1. Tab with no selection → insert spaces (already works)
2. Tab with selection → indent all selected lines
3. Shift+Tab → dedent

Auto-indent on Enter requires inspecting the current line's leading whitespace and prepending it to the new line.

New command types needed: `indentLines`, `dedentLines`. Modify `insertNewline` to support auto-indent.
d.title: Indentation: indent/dedent selections, auto-indent on newline

d.description:
Standard comment toggling:

- **Cmd+/** — Toggle line comment on current line or all selected lines
- **Cmd+Shift+A** — Toggle block comment around selection (lower priority)

Requires knowledge of the language's comment syntax (e.g. `//` for JS/TS, `#` for Python). Could use a simple language→comment-prefix map or pull from syntax highlighting metadata.

New command type: `toggleComment` (and optionally `toggleBlockComment`).
d.title: Comment toggling: Cmd+/ to toggle line comments

d.description:
Standard find/replace bindings:

- **Cmd+F** — Open find bar / initiate find
- **Cmd+H** — Open find & replace
- **Cmd+G** or **F3** — Find next occurrence
- **Cmd+Shift+G** or **Shift+F3** — Find previous occurrence
- **Cmd+E** — Use selection as search term
- **Enter** (in find bar) — Find next
- **Escape** — Close find bar

Go to line:
- **Ctrl+G** — Go to line number

This is a larger feature involving UI (find bar) and search logic. The core needs:
- Text search within buffer/multibuffer (plain text + regex)
- Match highlighting
- Navigation between matches
- Replace / replace all

Consider whether find state lives in Editor or in a separate FindController.
d.title: Find & Replace: Cmd+F, Cmd+H, find next/prev, go to line

d.description:
Standard multi-cursor bindings:

- **Cmd+D** — Select next occurrence of current word/selection (add cursor)
- **Cmd+Shift+L** — Select all occurrences of current selection
- **Opt+Click** — Add cursor at click position
- **Cmd+Opt+Up / Cmd+Opt+Down** — Add cursor above/below

Currently only a single cursor/selection is supported. This requires:
1. Selection model supports multiple selections (array of Selection)
2. All editing commands operate on each selection independently
3. Overlapping selections merge automatically
4. Cursor rendering draws multiple carets

This is a significant architectural change to the selection model. Consider implementing in phases:
- Phase 1: Multiple selections data model + rendering
- Phase 2: Opt+Click and Cmd+Opt+Up/Down (explicit placement)
- Phase 3: Cmd+D / Cmd+Shift+L (occurrence-based)
d.title: Multi-cursor editing: Cmd+D, Cmd+Shift+L, Opt+Click

d.description:
Standard bracket/pair bindings:

- **Auto-close pairs** — Typing `(`, `[`, `{`, `"`, `'`, `\`` auto-inserts the closing character
- **Type-over closing** — Typing `)` when cursor is before `)` skips over it instead of inserting
- **Cmd+Shift+\\** — Jump to matching bracket
- **Backspace on empty pair** — Deleting `(` when followed by `)` removes both

Surround (lower priority):
- Typing a bracket/quote with text selected wraps the selection

Pair characters: `() [] {} "" '' \`\``

New behaviors needed in insertText and deleteBackward commands, plus a new `jumpToMatchingBracket` command.
d.title: Bracket pair handling: auto-close, match navigation, surround

d.description:
Standard macOS text system bindings (inherited from Emacs, work in all native Mac text fields):

- **Ctrl+A** — Move to beginning of line (same as Cmd+Left / Home)
- **Ctrl+E** — Move to end of line (same as Cmd+Right / End)
- **Ctrl+K** — Kill (cut) from cursor to end of line
- **Ctrl+Y** — Yank (paste) killed text (uses a separate kill ring, not system clipboard)
- **Ctrl+T** — Transpose characters (swap character before and after cursor)
- **Ctrl+O** — Insert newline after cursor without moving cursor (open line)
- **Ctrl+D** — Delete forward (same as Delete key)
- **Ctrl+H** — Delete backward (same as Backspace)
- **Ctrl+B** — Move backward one character (same as Left arrow)
- **Ctrl+F** — Move forward one character (same as Right arrow)
- **Ctrl+N** — Move down one line (same as Down arrow)
- **Ctrl+P** — Move up one line (same as Up arrow)

Some of these are aliases for existing commands (Ctrl+A = Home, etc.) and may only need key mapping. Others (Ctrl+K kill, Ctrl+T transpose, Ctrl+O open line) need new command types.

Note: These should NOT be bound if a Ctrl+ combo conflicts with other bindings on the platform. Check that the demo app isn't already intercepting these.
d.title: macOS text system bindings: Ctrl+A/E/K/T/O (Emacs-style)

d.description:
Standard scroll-without-cursor-move bindings:

- **Ctrl+Up** — Scroll viewport up one line (cursor stays)
- **Ctrl+Down** — Scroll viewport down one line (cursor stays)
- **Cmd+Up** / **Cmd+Down** — Already mapped to jump to buffer start/end (keep as-is)

Also consider:
- **Scroll to center cursor** — Ctrl+L or similar, scrolls viewport so cursor line is centered
- **Scroll to cursor if off-screen** — Automatic behavior when cursor moves out of viewport (may already be handled)

These are viewport-only operations that don't change editor state—they only adjust the scroll offset. May need a new command type like `scrollViewport` or could be handled purely in the renderer/app layer.
d.title: Scroll commands: scroll viewport without moving cursor

d.description:
Standard text transformation bindings (often behind menus, but good to support):

- **Cmd+Shift+U** — Transform selection to UPPERCASE (or cycle case)
- **Cmd+U** — Transform selection to lowercase (varies by editor)
- **Title Case** — Capitalize first letter of each word

VS Code uses the command palette for these rather than default keybindings. Consider whether these should be commands available for binding rather than having default bindings.

New command type: `transformCase` with modes `upper`, `lower`, `title`.
d.title: Text transformation: uppercase, lowercase, title case

d.description:
Standard selection expansion bindings:

- **Cmd+L** — Select current line (repeat to extend to next line)
- **Cmd+Shift+K** — Delete line (already implemented)
- **Ctrl+Shift+Cmd+Right** — Expand selection (smart select: word → expression → statement → block → function)
- **Ctrl+Shift+Cmd+Left** — Shrink selection (reverse of expand)

`selectLineAt` already exists for triple-click. Cmd+L would need a `selectLine` command that works from keyboard and supports extending on repeat.

Smart expand/shrink selection is lower priority and requires syntax tree awareness.

Note: Some of these may already be achievable with existing commands (e.g., Shift+Down from line start effectively selects a line). The issue is about having dedicated, ergonomic bindings.
d.title: Selection expansion: Cmd+L select line, expand/shrink selection
